zh.lifeSummary: 當Johann Christof Wolf於1575年12月15日出生在德國巴登-爾特波格藍德可萊斯 卡爾茲盧斯圖斯塔福特時，他的父親Hans Jacob Martin Wolff32歲，母親Katharina Lucia Bientzlin32歲。他和Anna Barbara Mehbreys至少有1個女兒。他去世於1638年3月17日在德國巴登-爾特波格藍德可萊斯 卡爾茲盧斯圖史額可享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Johann Christof Wolf于1575年12月15日出生在德国巴登-爾特波格藍德可萊斯 卡爾茲盧斯圖斯塔福特时，他的父亲Hans Jacob Martin Wolff32岁，母亲Katharina Lucia Bientzlin32岁。他和Anna Barbara Mehbreys至少有1个女儿。他去世于1638年3月17日在德国巴登-爾特波格藍德可萊斯 卡爾茲盧斯圖史額可享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Wolf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English, Dutch, and Danish: from a short form of the various ancient Germanic compound names with the first element wolf ‘wolf’, or a byname or nickname with this meaning, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a wolf. The wolf was native throughout the forests of Europe, including Britain, until comparatively recently. In ancient and medieval times it played an important role in ancient Germanic mythology, being regarded as one of the sacred beasts of Woden. The surname of German origin is also found in many other parts of Europe, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Hungary, Czechia, Slovakia, Slovenia, and Croatia, often as a German translation of local equivalents. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Farkas , Czech and Slovak Vlk , Slovenian Volk , Ukrainian and Slovenian Vovk , Slovenian, Croatian, and Serbian Vuk , and also Czech, Slovenian, Croatian, Slovak, and Hungarian Volf . In part, this is a Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) surname, originating from the Kočevsko region in Lower Carniola, Slovenia (see Kocevar ). Compare De Wolf , Wolfe , Wolff , Woolf , Woulfe , and Wulf .

Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish male personal name Volf meaning ‘wolf’, which is associated with the Hebrew personal name Binyamin (see Benjamin ). This association stems from Jacob's dying words ‘Benjamin shall ravin as a wolf: in the morning he shall devour the prey, and at night he shall divide the spoil’ (Genesis 49:27). Compare Volf .
Irish: variant of   Woulfe  .

variants.name: Volkov
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Igor, Sergey, Oleg, Vladimir, Boris, Grigoriy, Sergei, Aleksandr, Anatoliy, Arkadiy, Emiliya, Evgeni, Vladislav.  Jewish Baruch, Naum, Ruvim.
Russian: patronymic from the old personal name or nickname Volk, from volk ‘wolf’.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name from any of the places called Volkovo or Volki, now in Belarus, which in turn are derived from Russian volk ‘wolf’.

variants.name: Littlewolf
meanings: Native American (also Little Wolf): translation into English of a personal name such as Chippewa Ma'iingaans, from a diminutive of ma'iingan ‘wolf’, or Lakota Sioux Šuŋgmanitu Čikala, composed of šuŋgmanitu ‘wolf’ and čikala ‘little’. See also Wolf 4.
Native American (Cheyenne): from a mistranslation into English of the Cheyenne personal name O'kohomoxhaahketa ‘Little Coyote’ (see Littlecoyote ).

History: O'kohomoxhaahketa (Anglicized as e.g. Ohcumgache) was the name of a Northern Cheyenne chief (c. 1820–1904) who, in 1878, led a heroic escape of his people from confinement in OK back to MT, the Cheyenne homeland. He is known in English as Little Wolf. He had two sons, but the name Little Wolf was carried on as a surname by his nephew.

variants.name: Wilf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Emanuel, Gittel, Ilan.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): from a personal name, a Yiddish dialect variant of   Wolf  .

variants.name: Woelfel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Erwin, Erna, Florian, Kurt, Othmar, Ulrike.
German (mainly Wölfel): from a pet form of the personal name Wolf or any of the compound names beginning with this element.

variants.name: Wolfanger
meanings: German: variant of Wolffanger ‘wolf catcher’, an occupational name for a trapper who caught wolves (see Wolf , Fanger ).

variants.name: Farkas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Laszlo, Tibor, Attila, Sandor, Zoltan, Gabor, Geza, Janos, Istvan, Miklos, Bela, Csaba.  Jewish Arie, Chaim, Moshe, Liat, Mayer, Zev, Ari, Asher, Eliezer, Faigy, Fishel, Mendel.

Hungarian: from the old personal name Farkas, derived from farkas ‘wolf’. It is also a nickname derived from the same word. The surname Farkas is also found in Romania and Slovakia (see also 2 below).

Slovak, Czech, Serbian, Croatian, Slovenian, and Rusyn (from Vojvodina in Serbia) (Farkaš): from a Slavicized form of Hungarian farkas ‘wolf’, used either as a nickname or a personal name; or, in part, an adaptation of the cognate Hungarian surname Farkas (see 1 above).

variants.name: Lupu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Cornel, Florin, Mihai, Petru, Traian, Viorel, Alejandro, Corina, Costica, Elena, Ilie.
Romanian: nickname from lupu ‘wolf’ (from Latin lupus). It may also be from the old personal name Lupu with the same meaning.
Jewish (from Romania): translation (see above) of the personal name   Wolf   ‘wolf’.

variants.name: Volf
meanings: Czech, Slovenian, Croatian, Slovak, and Hungarian: from German   Wolf   1, which was often used as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic nicknames or surnames. Compare   Bolf  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Wolf   2.

variants.name: Vuk
meanings: Slovenian: nickname from vuk, a dialect variant of volk ‘wolf’. Compare Wolf .
Croatian, Serbian, and Bosniak: from the Croatian and Serbian personal name Vuk ‘wolf’; or a nickname from a Croatian, Serbian, and Bosnian word with this meaning (compare 1 above).

variants.name: Wolfe
meanings: English, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Wolf   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. This is also a common spelling in Ireland (see also   Woulfe  ), particularly in counties Cork and Limerick, while in Germany this surname is very rare.
Americanized form of various other surnames meaning ‘wolf’, e.g. Hungarian   Farkas  , Czech and Slovak   Vlk  , Slovenian and other Slavic   Volk  ,   Vovk  , and   Vuk   (see   Wolf  ).

nameMeanings.name: Johann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vernacular form of   Johannes .

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Christof
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Greek Andreas, Christos, Anastase, Costas, Ioannis, Vasilios, Anastasios, Antonios, Charalambos, Christakis, Evangelos, Kosta.
Greek: patronymic from the genitive case of the personal name   Christos  .

variants.name: Christos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Greek Constantine, Dinos, Spero.

Greek: from the personal name Christos, which is a short form of Christodoulos ‘servant of Christ’ and Christoforos ‘Christ-bearing’ (see Christopher ). In most cases, however, the surname Christos is an (American) shortened form of patronymics derived from these personal names, such as Christakos , Christodoulakis, Christodoulopoulos, Christoforidis, and Christopoulos , and a shortened form of compound surnames formed with the personal name Christos, as for example Christovasilis ‘Basil, son of Christos’, Christogeorgis ‘George, son of Christos’, Christogiannis ‘John, son of Christos’. Compare Chris 1, Christ 3, and Christo 1.

en.lifeSummary: When Johann Christof Wolf was born on 15 December 1575, in Staffort, Stutensee, Karlsruhe, Baden-Württemberg, Germany, his father, Hans Jacob Martin Wolff, was 32 and his mother, Katharina Lucia Bientzlin, was 32. He had at least 1 daughter with Anna Barbara Mehbreys. He died on 17 March 1638, in Spöck, Stutensee, Karlsruhe, Baden-Württemberg, Germany, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Johann Christof Wolf est né le 15 décembre 1575, à Staffort, Stutensee, Landkreis Karlsruhe, Baden-Württemberg, Allemagne, son père, Hans Jacob Martin Wolff, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Katharina Lucia Bientzlin, avait 32 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Anna Barbara Mehbreys. Il est décédé le 17 mars 1638, à Spöck, Stutensee, Landkreis Karlsruhe, Baden-Württemberg, Allemagne, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Johann Christof Wolf am 15. Dezember 1575 in Staffort, Stutensee, Landkreis Karlsruhe, Baden-Württemberg, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hans Jacob Martin Wolff 32 und seine Mutter Katharina Lucia Bientzlin 32. Er hatte mit Anna Barbara Mehbreys mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb am 17. März 1638 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Spöck, Stutensee, Landkreis Karlsruhe, Baden-Württemberg, Deutschland.

ko.lifeSummary: Johann Christof Wolf(이)가 1575년 12월 15일 독일 바데-wu르테버그 랜드크레이스 칼스루헤 스투텐시 스태포트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hans Jacob Martin Wolff(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Katharina Lucia Bientzlin(은)는 32세였다. 그는 Anna Barbara Mehbreys(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1638년 3월 17일 독일 바데-wu르테버그 랜드크레이스 칼스루헤 스투텐시 스poc크에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.
